D11 ComputationalL2 UnderstandReference

Palette Extraction — Attention-Style Weighting

Modern palette tools (Adobe Color, Coolors) weight pixels by saliency before clustering — important regions (faces, objects) dominate the palette over background

Naive k-means weights every pixel equally — a noisy sky dominates a portrait. Attention-weighted variants use a saliency map (CNN or simple centre-bias prior) to upweight foreground pixels. Result: extracted palette reflects the subject, not the background area.

Slide attention strength (0 = uniform, 1 = subject-only).
